Travel Grants

  • Home
  • Attend
  • Travel Grants

IEEE TPS 2025

The 7th IEEE International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications

Nov. 11-14, 2025, Pittsburgh, PA, USA

Co-located with IEEE CIC 2025 and IEEE CogMI 2025

SIGIR Student Travel Grant Program

The SIGIR Student Travel Grant Program provides funding for students to help cover the cost of attending SIGIR-sponsored conferences including SIGIR, CIKM, CHIIR, ICTIR, JCDL and WSDM. This year, only ONE author of each accepted full/short/demo/DC paper need to register as “Author-Main Conference” in CIC 2025 and the SIGIR Travel Grant Program has decided to cover the “Author-Main Conference” registration fee for any student AUTHOR who registers as “Author-Main Conference” in the CIC 2025 registration system. The grants will be given in the form of reimbursements, so students will need to cover their costs upfront.

Eligibility

To be eligible, the student MUST satisfy both of the following two criteria:
  • be an AUTHOR of a full, short, demo, and/or DC paper
  • be in a position where they need to pay the “Author-Main Conference” registration rate to attend CIC 2025.
Note that student authors of a workshop paper are NOT eligible.

Application

To apply, please complete this form for CIC 2025. Note that:
  • The form for CIC 2025 expires on July 25, 2020 Anywhere on Earth (AoE).
  • These awards will be processed as reimbursements by the ACM and awardees will need to provide receipts for the “Author-Main Conference” fee.
  • Awardees will receive instructions on how to be reimbursed after the conference.

Contact

Jaime Arguello,
SIGIR Student Travel Grant Program Chair
Email: jarguell@email.unc.edu
If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to contact us.
